CONTENT.Honours: Viktoria PlzeňCzech First League: 2012–13,  2014–15Slavia PragueCzech First League: 2016–17
CONTENT.Club career: Tecl served as captain of Jihlava early in his career. He joined Viktoria Plzeň in January 2013, scoring for the club in his first friendly game after just 21 minutes. On 27 May 2017, he scored two goals in a 4–0 victory against Zbrojovka Brno in the final matchday of the 2016–17 Czech First League that clinched the title for his team. He scored two goals as Slavia Prague won the 2017–18 Czech Cup final 3–1 against Jablonec on 9 May 2018. Tecl played his final game of his professional career on 27 May 2024, coming on as a substitute in the 82nd minute for Slavia in a 4–0 win against Mladá Boleslav.
CONTENT.Personal life: On 25 December 2016, Tecl welcomed his first son, Matěj.
CONTENT.External links: Stanislav Tecl at FAČR (in Czech)Stanislav Tecl at SoccerwayProfile at Slavia Prague websiteUEFA profile
CONTENT.International career: Having represented the Czech Republic at youth level, Tecl debuted for the senior squad in a 2–0 friendly victory against Turkey in February 2013.

SUMMARY: Stanislav Tecl (born 1 September 1990) is a Czech former professional footballer who played as a forward. He played most games for Slavia Prague in the Czech First League.
